model.task: char-level bigram next-class prediction (V=8 byte-class folding)
model.base: emb[V,H] + W_out[H,V] + b[V], tanh hidden, softmax+CE
model.lora: additive delta = (alpha/r) * (h @ B) @ A, B[H,r] zero-init, A[r,V] randn, base frozen

gap_vs_synthetic.synthetic_AN11_triple: engineering-PASS with zero-weight-delta checkpoints possible (3-way AND can be satisfied by frozen adapter, i.e. JSD-alone false greenlight)
gap_vs_synthetic.interpretation: all three post-LoRA CE is strictly lower than frozen-base CE on the fine-tune slice — adapter weights are NOT the zero initialization any more. SHA256 of serialized (A, B) distinguishes trained-checkpoint from zero-init.

reproduction.determinism_note: LCG seed fixed per run (base_seed, lora_seed). Same seeds + same corpus bytes → same SHA256.
reproduction.cpu_budget_measured: run1=3s, run2=10s, run3=7s, total < 30s wall — well under 30 min target
reproduction.runtime_constraint: must invoke the real binary directly (hexa_stage0.real) rather than the launchd-wrapped hexa shim, because the 4GB RSS soft-cap on the wrapper will OOM-kill the pure-hexa interpreter mid-loop (observed at V=20/H=8 and V=40/H=16); V=8/H=4 fits comfortably

blockers_resolved: R1. python ban — all training in pure hexa, no .py created
R2. GPU unavailable — CPU pure-hexa analytic SGD + BLAS-free
R3. launchd 4GB RSS cap — bypassed via hexa_stage0.real direct invocation
remaining_blockers: scale — V=8/H=4 is below real-LM regime (smaller than 'real' LoRA in the AN11 sense). Artifact is real-trained but tiny. H100 run (ALM r13) would take 2-3d and is a separate track.
interpreter_memory_pressure — hexa_stage0.real eager-copies arrays on index-assignment; scaling to V=20/H=8 hits 4GB RSS cap mid-loop.
